    Teaching

    MAST10024 Mathematics and Statistics for Biomedicine. Semester 1, 2026

    Undergraduate subject, School of Mathematics and Statistics, The University of Melbourne, 1900

    This subject is a first-year undergraduate core elective for the Bachelor of Biomedicine (and also available as breadth for other degrees). In Semester 1, 2026, there are approximately 100 enrolments. The course covers the mathematics behind mass action kinetics, enzyme kinetics, hypothesis testing, linear regression and epidemic modelling. My contributions to the material have been to update assessment (including adding a ‘Part A’ to every assignment, asking students to reflect on course materials), add new tutorial questions and reorder material to be better aligned, and create new revision materials.

    MAST20034 Critical Thinking with Data. Semester 2, 2025

    Undergraduate subject, School of Mathematics and Statistics, The University of Melbourne, 1900

    This is a second-year undergraduate statistics subject that is a core subject for the Business Analytics major in the Bachelor of Commerce. This subject ran for the first time in Semester 2, 2025. I co-coordinated with Dr. Paul Fijn. The course content includes study design and best practice for collecting data, working with and reporting on data and interpreting reports of data. We focussed on giving students agency with their in-class tasks and assessment. This involved creating a collection of case studied for students to choose from (supported by funding from a Teaching Support Scheme Grant). Paul and I are very interested in Universal Design for Learning and applied many of these principles to the design and implementation of the subject. Feel free to ask me more about this!
